message:
fix(cli,tests): format resolve_optional_string_choice and rewrite watch E2E to use stderr startup detection

The watch export E2E test was racing against filesystem polling — it used
a file-existence check (wait_for_file) that was unreliable on slow
machines. Now it reads the watch process's stderr line-by-line until the
"Watching " startup line appears, then signals the modifier thread to
mutate the beads file, eliminating the race.

- cli.rs: reformat resolve_optional_string_choice signature for
  line-length compliance (no logic change)
- e2e_export_pages.rs: replace wait_for_file polling with
  wait_for_watch_startup that reads stderr lines until startup is
  advertised; spawn watch via ProcessCommand with piped stderr
  instead of assert_cmd; use mpsc channel to synchronize modifier
  thread start with watch readiness; add explicit flush after
  appending the test issue

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -632,7 +632,10 @@ fn resolve_output_format_choice(
     Ok(cli_format)
 }
 
-fn resolve_optional_string_choice(cli_value: Option<&str>, env_value: Option<&str>) -> Option<String> {
+fn resolve_optional_string_choice(
+    cli_value: Option<&str>,
+    env_value: Option<&str>,
+) -> Option<String> {
     cli_value
         .map(str::trim)
         .filter(|value| !value.is_empty())
